Theoretical guarantees for the method include Fisher consistency and excess risk bounds, with empirical results showing superior speed and performance.

items.title: Variational Consensus Monte Carlo for Bayesian Mixture
items.summary: Presents a comprehensive pipeline for Bayesian mixture model inference in federated learning settings where data cannot be fully shared. It utilizes an extended Variational Consensus Monte Carlo (CMC) approach to estimate local posteriors independently across data silos and aggregate them. Key contributions include adapting variational CMC for mixtures that infer both the number of clusters and all parameters, even without assuming conjugacy. The method also introduces novel cluster-matching algorithms for cross-silo settings where not all clusters are present locally. A simulation study validates this framework, showing superior recovery of small clusters compared to standard pooled MCMC methods when local data reflects underlying structure, using electronic health record data as an example.

items.title: AURA: Adaptive Uncertainty-aware Refinement for LLM-as-a-Judge Auditing
items.summary: AURA introduces an adaptive framework to audit LLMs used as judges for open-ended generation tasks. It addresses the limitation of existing pipelines that require reliable, pre-existing supervision signals, which are often unavailable in practice. AURA operates on pairwise LLM-as-a-judge decisions, utilizing limited human verification data. The core mechanism is iteratively learning a "human-consistency signal" to progressively refine trust in the judge model. This allows the system to prioritize uncertain comparisons for targeted human review, ensuring robust evaluation with scarce supervision.

items.title: Stochastic Linear Contextual Bandits with Bounded Noise: A Set-Membership Approach
items.summary: Addresses stochastic linear contextual bandits (SLCB) under the specific assumption of bounded reward noise. Existing literature often assumes weaker sub-Gaussian noise, yielding $\tilde{O}(\sqrt{T})$ regret bounds. The paper proposes a novel algorithm named SME-OFU that integrates set-membership estimation with optimism in the face of uncertainty. This new approach achieves an improved regret bound of $O(\log T)$. Empirical simulations validate that SME-OFU outperforms benchmark algorithms when reward noise is bounded.

items.title: AK-MCS-C2 : Active Kriging Monte Carlo Simulation method with conformal certification for failure probability estimation
items.summary: A novel active-learning framework combines Active Kriging Monte Carlo simulation with conformal prediction for structural reliability failure probability estimation. The approach uses an adaptive cross-conformal strategy optimized for small samples, employing a J+GP conformal estimator. This method provides distribution-free guarantees on prediction errors, surpassing standard AK-MCS techniques. Such improvements enhance the accuracy and robustness of failure estimates, particularly in rare-event regimes near the limit-state surface. Numerical comparisons confirm the effectiveness of this enhanced uncertainty quantification framework.

items.title: Off-Policy Evaluation for Missingness-Aware Policies in MDPs with Rewards Missing Not at Random
items.summary: Investigates off-policy evaluation (OPE) in Markov decision processes when immediate rewards are Missing Not at Random (MNAR), which violates ignorability and causes selection bias. To address this, the work formalizes a reward-dependent propensity model utilizing future states as shadow variables to identify the full-data conditional mean reward. A novel bridge function is introduced to recover this conditional mean reward via a min-max procedure, avoiding explicit MNAR modeling. An accompanying Fitted-Q-Evaluation-style estimator propagates these recovered rewards and allows target policies to incorporate missingness indicators. The developed estimator demonstrates consistency and finite-sample error bounds, showing strong performance on simulated and real sepsis data.

items.title: Statistical Properties of Training & Generalization
items.summary: Deep learning has achieved superior real-world performance despite contradicting classical statistical intuitions. This paper examines key features and unexpected aspects of deep learning using a physics-informed viewpoint. It carefully identifies and justifies the various choices involved in constructing deep learning models. Specifically, it reviews neural scaling laws. The discussion further explores how these scaling laws interact with constraints and inductive biases when applying machine learning to physical problems.

items.title: SSH-Net: A Deep Neural Network for Predicting Failure Time Distribution Functions under Competing Risks with Application to GPU Data
items.summary: SSH-Net, a Structured Segmented Hazard Deep Neural Network, is proposed for predicting failure time distributions under competing risks. It addresses limitations in existing deep learning models by structuring the network architecture to match complex physical system data. The model utilizes separate sub-networks allowing different covariate groups to independently influence failure prediction within a cause-specific framework. SSH-Net outputs cause-specific hazard functions and minimizes a penalized log-likelihood loss function. Its performance was validated using simulation studies and applied successfully to predict GPU failure time distributions using Titan data.
